title: Peristylus holttumii
intro: Peristylus holttumii, also known as the Holttum's caladenia, is a species of orchid that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Peristylus holttumii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Peristylus holttumii is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It is a genus of orchids, specifically within the subtribe Orchidinae.
